rationale.R1.2: A material internal contradiction exists: contributions are framed as 'learning-based' and contribution (ii) claims a 'clarification-generation policy that improves with user feedback,' but the implementation is a prompt-engineering pipeline over a *frozen* LLM with no weight updates or online learning. This logical inconsistency is load-bearing if technical review is in scope.
rationale.R1.3: The concluding paragraph asserts 'substantial improvements' and 'validate our claim,' but the evidence rests on 40 hand-authored commands drawn from a single instrumented kitchen. The jump from n=40, one environment, one user-judge to the general claim about robot assistants 'inherent in human language' is a significant evidence-claim gap — load-bearing if technical review is in scope.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is the most natural reading of a prose-quality check; writing polish is one plausible load-bearing interpretation of the delegation, making it a candidate primary responsibility alongside R1.2/R1.3.
